rulings.Case Title: GENE LEPORE VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION / Navistar, Inc'S Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 29, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Navistar, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. As to Issue One, the motion for summary adjudication of the third cause of action is granted to the extent that cause of action alleged that defendant owed a duty for products neither manufactured, supplied nor distributed by International Harvester/Navistar. (O'Neil v. Crane Co. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 335.) The motion as to the third cause of action is denied to the extent it alleged that defendant owed a duty for products (brake linings/clutch components) that it incorporated into its original equipment or that it supplied or distributed as aftermarket replacement parts. The Court may summarily adjudicate this issue of duty. (C.C.P. § 437c(f)(1).) In addition to the parties' cited authority on this topic, the parties should be prepared to discuss Linden Partners v. Wilshire Linden Associates (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 508 and Public Utilities Commission v. Superior Court (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 364. Plaintiffs did not substantively oppose this issue nor did they submit evidence to create a triable issue of material fact whether defendant owed a duty for products neither manufactured, supplied nor distributed by International Harvester/Navistar. As to Issue Two (punitive damages), the motion is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The deposition testimony of James Shuman (Exhibits O and Y), the caution label (Exhibit S), and the service manual (Exhibit BB) create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. The Court will not consider plaintiffs' late-filed March 20, 2017 opposition papers. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
